title: Gene Editing
puzzle: How can scientists change a living organism by editing its DNA?
insight: Gene editing uses molecular tools to target specific DNA sequences, making it possible to remove, alter, or insert genetic instructions.
key_ideas: Tools like CRISPR can target chosen sequences.
Precision matters because changes can have far-reaching effects.
mind_blower: Editing DNA means changing not just one trait, but the instruction language cells use to build life.
next_curiosity: If gene editing becomes routine, how should societies decide where to draw limits?
narration_text: How can scientists change a living organism by editing its DNA? Gene editing uses molecular tools to target specific DNA sequences, making it possible to remove, alter, or insert genetic instructions. DNA stores biological instructions. Tools like CRISPR can target chosen sequences. Precision matters because changes can have far-reaching effects. Editing DNA means changing not just one trait, but the instruction language cells use to build life. If gene editing becomes routine, how should societies decide where to draw limits?
